'''Jeremy Donougher''' (born 28 November 1969) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played at club level for the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]] and the [[Bradford Bulls]].

==Career==
Donougher made his first grade debut for South Sydney in round 2 of the 1993 season against [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles|Manly]] at [[Brookvale Oval]].  Donougher played for Souths in their upset 1994 [[Tooheys Challenge Cup]] final victory over [[Brisbane Broncos|Brisbane]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rabbitohs.com.au/news/2018/04/24/the-giant-killers/|title=The Giant Killers|website=www.rabbitohs.com.au|date=15 December 2023 }}</ref>

Donougher joined [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]] in December 1995.<ref>{{cite web|last1=Hadfield|first1=Dave|title=Rugby League: Wigan vs Bath on League's agenda|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-league-wigan-vs-bath-on-leagues-agenda-1524369.html |archive-url=https://ghostarchive.org/archive/20220608/https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-league-wigan-vs-bath-on-leagues-agenda-1524369.html |archive-date=8 June 2022 |url-access=subscription |url-status=live|website=The Independent|accessdate=30 July 2014|date=6 December 1995}}</ref> In 1996, he scored a hat trick of tries for the club against [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]],<ref>{{cite news|title=Jeremy charges in for Bulls|url=http://www.thefreelibrary.com/Jeremy+charges+in+for+Bulls.-a061331089|accessdate=30 July 2014|work=The Mirror|date=1 April 1996}}</ref> and played in the [[1996 Challenge Cup]] Final defeat against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]]. He missed out on the cup final rematch a year later due to injury.<ref>{{cite news|last1=Richards|first1=Martin|title=Bradford Cup Blow|url=http://www.thefreelibrary.com/BRADFORD+CUP+BLOW.-a061117595|accessdate=30 July 2014|publisher=The Mirror|date=25 March 1997}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.ssralmanac.com/mobile/players_d/player_donougher_jeremy.htm|title=South Sydney Rabbitohs Rugby League Player Report - Jeremy Donougher|website=www.ssralmanac.com}}</ref>
